title: Carthamus tenuis
intro: Carthamus tenuis, also known as Safflower, is an annual herbaceous plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, characterized by its bright yellow or orange flowers and slender stems. The plant is valued for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help in treating conditions such as fever, skin disorders, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Carthamus tenuis include carthamidin, carthamin, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and ointments, as well as being used as a food coloring and a source of edible oil, particularly in traditional Chinese and Ayurvedic medicine.
